task: A friend texted asking to split a shared expense, and you’re deciding how to answer while keeping things simple. You want to respond in a way that won’t turn into a back-and-forth, even if you end up suggesting a different arrangement.

turns.agent_response:
You could say something like:

“Sure — I can send my share. If it’s easier, I’m also fine with [your alternative arrangement].”

If you want it even more closed-ended:

“Yep, I’ll send my share now. If you’d prefer a different split next time, just let me know.”

If you want, I can help you phrase it more casually, politely, or firmly.

user_simulator.text:
Too generic. I need something I can just text בלי all the soft stuff.

More like:

“Yeah, I’ll send it over. Next time let’s just do separate.”

Or if I’m not paying right away:

“Can’t do it that way. I’ll cover my part, but let’s split it different next time.”

Short. Done. No little back-and-forth.

turns.agent_response:
Got it — here are a few blunt, text-ready options:

- “Yeah, I’ll send it over. Next time let’s just do separate.”
- “I’ll pay my part. Let’s do separate next time.”
- “Can’t do it that way. I’ll cover mine, but next time let’s split it differently.”
- “I’m good to pay my share. Separate next time.”
- “I’ll send mine now. Next time, just split it at the time.”

If you want, I can make them:
- even colder
- more casual
- more direct but still friendly

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short, no drama.
